page: Amazon Web Services r7a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results show strong performance across multiple domains. The server achieves top 25% results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong write bandwidth for large blocks and a strong cached read bandwidth for small blocks, though uncached large block read bandwidth falls into the average category. In database and key-value store operations, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities, processing 78.07 million Redis SET operations per second and scoring in the top 25% for Passmark database operations.

The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (F, VNNI, and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. These instructions contribute to top 10% performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation for small 135M models, and top 25% text generation for medium 7B models. However, static web serving throughput and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression remain in the average tier. The lack of local NVMe storage means all storage must be network-attached, which is a key architectural tradeoff.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.8xlarge offers a high-density DDR5 memory configuration paired with dedicated physical cores, making it suitable for memory-bound enterprise workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is positioned for database deployments, in-memory caching, software compilation, and small-to-medium scale language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in CPU and memory tests, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark database operations, alongside a top 10% tier for small-model LLM inference. Tradeoffs include average uncached large-block read memory bandwidth and the absence of local storage. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive database operations, Redis caching, software compilation, and low-latency text generation workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong database and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong database benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding database and in-memory caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz.
256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Strong database performance with 78.07 million Redis SET operations per second.
Top-tier prompt processing and text generation for small LLM models.
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions.
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation.
